title: Compliance Trending — Regulatory Change Feed Impact Score on In-Scope Controls

compliance.assurance_rationale: Phase-D retagged from meta-multi placeholder to a concrete ISO 27001 clause. §9.1 Monitoring/measurement — regulatory-change impact scoring is a form of monitoring the external context. Assurance level set to 'contributing' pending SME review per Phase E of the plan.

Phase-D retagged from meta-multi placeholder to a concrete SOC 2 clause. SOC 2 CC3.1 Risk Assessment — regulatory change is a primary risk-assessment input. Assurance level set to 'contributing' pending SME review per Phase E of the plan.

app: HTTP Event Collector from regulatory intelligence vendor or manual curator tool
spl:
index=grc sourcetype="reg:change_item" earliest=-730d
| eval eff=strptime(effective_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where eff>now() OR eff>relative_time(now(),"-90d@d")
| timechart span=30d sum(impact_score) as rolling_impact by jurisdiction
| trendline sma3(rolling_impact) as impact_trend
value: Laws and supervisory guidance change continuously. Scoring incoming regulatory change items by how many in-scope controls they touch—and trending that score—helps compliance prioritize policy updates, training, and control amendments before effective dates.
implementation: (1) Maintain mapping table from `impacted_control_ids` to owners; (2) human validates `impact_score`—do not fully automate legal severity; (3) integrate effective-date countdown alerts; (4) deduplicate vendor spam with `item_id`; (5) cross-link to policy management tickets.
visualization: Line chart (rolling_impact + impact_trend), Table (upcoming items), Single value (next-90d sum).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
